summary: Bryan Cantrill recounts Team DTrace's appearance on the Scoble Show, which took place the day after his younger son was born. The post is a lighthearted reflection on the interview, complete with celebratory cigars that led to a reprimand from building management. He also addresses the status of DTrace on the iPhone, noting that while it's not yet available on ARM, a port may be in development.
key_points: Team DTrace appeared on the Scoble Show for an interview
The interview occurred the day after Cantrill's son was born
Celebratory cigars in the office led to a conversation with building management
DTrace was not yet ported to iPhone/ARM but work may be underway
money_quotes: After the photo, we returned to our office and smoked the cigars – and then had an unplanned conversation with the building management about never again smoking cigars in the office.
I responded that I was done having kids, so they had nothing to worry about.
key_insight: Even significant technical achievements like DTrace can be celebrated with simple pleasures and humor, blending personal milestones with professional ones.
